mnemonics.title: SCAMPER Reminder
mnemonics.content: SCAMPER: Substitute things, Combine ideas, Adapt existing solutions, Modify by changing features, Put to other uses, Eliminate unnecessary elements, Reverse the approach.

mnemonics.title: Ideation Methods Classification
mnemonics.content: Ideation methods fall into three buckets: Intuitive (free-flow), Analytical (structured prompts), Visual (diagrams and sketches).

acronyms.expansion: Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to other uses, Eliminate, Reverse
acronyms.usage: Use as a checklist to transform existing ideas into new solutions during ideation sessions.
Use to generate rapid, diverse concepts by sketching quickly under time pressure.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a central circle with the main problem/idea, then branch out with related concepts using different colors. Think of it like a sun with rays expanding outward.
Think of it as a comic strip - each panel represents a step in the user journey, with stick figures showing actions and simple captions describing emotions.
Imagine sticky notes on a wall being grouped by color-coded themes - similar ideas naturally cluster together like sorting laundry.

analogies.analogy: Ideation is like planting many seeds in a garden. Not all will grow into plants, but you need many seeds to eventually harvest good produce. Divergent thinking plants seeds, convergent thinking chooses what to nurture.
Brainstorming is like skydiving - you must jump (generate ideas) before you can open your parachute (evaluate/select). Premature evaluation kills the creative momentum.
Crazy 8s is like speed dating for ideas - you quickly meet (sketch) many potential solutions in a short time, hoping to find a good match.
